articles.title: Stocks Rise as Chipmakers Extend Rebound, Oil Dips: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Asian stocks rose for the first time in four days as investors returned to chipmakers, driving a rebound in the sector after the recent selloff. Oil declined.

articles.title: Palm Slips from Month-High as Weak Crude Dents Biofuel Appeal
articles.summary: Palm oil fell from a near one-month high following weakness in the crude and vegetable oil complex, denting the appeal of biofuels.

articles.title: SBI Funds Rises 8.5% in Debut After $1 Billion India IPO

articles.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
articles.summary: Brent oil slipped after surging almost 6% over the previous two sessions, as traders weighed continued hostilities between the US and Iran against efforts to broker a new ceasefire.

articles.title: Turkish Airlines Exploring Acquisitions in Asia, South America
articles.summary: Turkish Airlines is exploring more acquisition opportunities as it looks to expand its global reach.

articles.title: Goldman Says Brent Could Top $120 If Hormuz Disruptions Persist
articles.summary: Brent could rally to more than $120 a barrel by the fourth quarter if disruptions of the Strait of Hormuz persist, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., although that’s not the bank’s base case.

articles.title: Japan Insurers Buy Most Super-Long Bonds in Three Years in June
articles.summary: Japanese insurance firms bought the most super-long government bonds in three years last month, adding to signs that demand from a key buyer is stabilizing.

articles.title: Iron Ore Drops as China Steel Losses Deepen, Hormuz Risks Rise
articles.summary: Iron ore fell for a second day as deteriorating profit margins at Chinese steel mills and continued US-Iran hostilities clouded the demand outlook.

articles.title: China Unleashes Broad State Support to Halt Tech Stock Selloff
articles.summary: China is stepping up efforts to stabilize its stock market, mobilizing a range of state-linked institutions to stem a tech-driven selloff. The chip-heavy STAR 50 index extended gains.

articles.title: Gold Rises on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Middle East Clashes
articles.summary: Gold rose on dip-buying as traders monitored developments in the Middle East for clues on the energy price impact on inflation.
